WorkContainers.Name: Expedition 'Arctic Horizon' - Grant #9088
WorkContainers.Description: Multi-institutional study on permafrost thawing and ancient pathogen modeling. Led by Prof. Steve Zissou.

WorkItems.Description: Ethics Review: Biological Sample Extraction Protocol

Comments.Body: The protocol for core drilling in the Svalbard zone lacks clear containment plans for unknown bacterial strains.
🤖 **GERDA Ethics Guard**: Compliance mismatch. 'Pathogen Containment' level 4 required for this region. Recommendation: Update safety equipment budget by 15% and resubmit.
Fuel allocation seems 23% higher than comparable expeditions. Need justification for the variance.
Risk assessment complete. All team members trained in polar bear deterrent protocols. Flare guns and electric fences ordered.

WorkContainers.Name: Grant 'Digital Heritage' - Archaeological Digitization
WorkContainers.Description: 3D digitization and preservation of endangered archaeological sites in the Middle East. Led by Dr. Indiana Jones.

WorkItems.Description: Ethics Review: Human Remains Handling Protocol

Comments.Body: Protocol requires revision. Local community consent must be documented before any remains are moved.
3D scanners calibrated for desert conditions. Team trained on LiDAR and photogrammetry. Ready for field deployment.

WorkItems.Description: Budget Allocation: Local Staff and Translators

Comments.Body: Insurance secured through Lloyd's of London. Coverage includes equipment, personnel, and site liability. Premium: $45,000.

WorkContainers.Name: Grant 'Quantum Computing for Climate' - Grant #9102
WorkContainers.Description: Applying quantum computing algorithms to climate modeling and carbon capture optimization. Led by Dr. Alan Turing.

Comments.Body: Initial benchmarks on IBM Q System One show 40% improvement over classical algorithms for small molecule simulation.

WorkItems.Description: Compute Budget: AWS Quantum Solutions Lab

Comments.Body: AWS Quantum Solutions Lab credits approved. $150,000 compute budget allocated for 12 months.

WorkContainers.Name: Expedition 'Galapagos Evolution' - Grant #9105
WorkContainers.Description: Long-term evolutionary study of Galapagos finches and marine iguanas. Led by Prof. Charles Darwin.

Comments.Body: Permit application submitted to Ministerio del Ambiente. Expected approval in 6-8 weeks.
Weather station installed and transmitting data. Solar panels working well. 24/7 data feed active.

WorkContainers.Name: Grant 'Analytical Engine' - CS Education Initiative
WorkContainers.Description: Computational thinking education program for underserved communities. Led by Dr. Ada Lovelace.

Comments.Body: Curriculum draft complete. 12 modules covering basics to data analysis. Focus on hands-on projects.

WorkContainers.Name: Grant 'Radioactive Decay' - Marie Curie Fellowship
WorkContainers.Description: Postdoctoral fellowship program for women in STEM. Named after Marie Curie.

Comments.Body: 47 applications received. Initial screening complete. 23 advancing to peer review.

WorkItems.Description: Budget Allocation: Stipends and Research Funds

Comments.Body: Budget model approved. 10 fellowships at $75k/year each. Research funds: $25k per fellow.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: URGENT: Archeological Find at Permafrost Core Station

Comments.Body: We found structures that appear to be pre-Holocene. We need an immediate ruling on whether we can continue drilling or if the site must be preserved under UN mandate.
🤖 **GERDA Policy Engine**: Automatic Halt Recommended. Coordinates match registered 'UNESCO Heritage Potential' zone. Routing to Ethics Officer and Legal Counsel.
Our field expenses for the Arctic expedition are 35% over budget. We need an emergency budget review or the expedition may need to be cut short.
🤖 **GERDA Budget Monitor**: Variance detected: +35.2% over approved budget. Root cause: Submersible fuel costs. Flagging for Financial Compliance Officer Ahmed.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Ethics Complaint: Animal Testing in Grant #9102

Comments.Body: An anonymous complaint was filed regarding animal testing protocols in our quantum computing project. We need an ethics review panel convened immediately.
We need a 6-month budget extension for the Galapagos expedition. El Nino delayed our field season by 4 months.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Grant Application: 'Deep Ocean Exploration' - Prof. Zissou

Comments.Body: Submitting new grant application for deep ocean exploration using autonomous submersibles. Budget: $2.4M over 3 years.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Data Sharing Request: Arctic Expedition Results

Comments.Body: Requesting access to permafrost data from Grant #9088 for our radioactivity correlation study. All data will be properly cited.
A researcher was injured by a marine iguana bite. They are receiving treatment locally. Need to review field safety protocols.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Report Request: Impact Metrics for All Active Grants

Comments.Body: The board needs a comprehensive impact report for all active grants. Include: publications, citations, policy outcomes, and media coverage.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Grant Application Review: 'Climate AI' - Dr. Turing

Comments.Body: Submitting application for Phase 2 funding. Phase 1 results are promising. Requesting $1.8M for 24 months.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Compliance Alert: Missing Financial Report - Grant #9088

Comments.Body: Q3 financial report for Grant #9088 is overdue by 15 days. This is a compliance violation per grant agreement section 4.2.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Equipment Failure: PCR Machine at Field Station

Comments.Body: The portable PCR machine at our Arctic field station has malfunctioned. We cannot process samples until it's repaired or replaced.

UnassignedWorkItems.Description: Request: Ethics Pre-approval for New Protocol

Comments.Body: We need ethics pre-approval for our new quantum algorithm testing protocol. No biological subjects involved, but we need to confirm data privacy compliance.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Arctic Sovereignty Laws (2025)
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Summary of research rights in international waters.

**Protocols:**
* No permanent structures without UN approval.
* Data sharing mandatory within 6 months of collection.
* Environmental impact fee: $500 per ton of ice displaced.
* Local community consent required for all land-based research.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Bio-Scrubbing Procedures
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Mandatory decontamination for all field samples.

**Steps:**
1. UV-C irradiation (120 seconds).
2. Chemical bath (Solvents A and B).
3. Vacuum seal for transport.

*Violations result in immediate grant termination.*

**Exceptions:**
* None. All samples must be processed.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Grant Budget Guidelines
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Guidelines for budgeting research grants.

**Allowable Expenses:**
* Personnel (salaries, benefits).
* Equipment (under $5k per item).
* Travel (economy class, per diem).
* Materials and supplies.
* Publication fees.

**Non-allowable:**
* Alcohol.
* Entertainment.
* Personal equipment.
* Overhead > 15%.

**Reporting:**
* Quarterly financial reports.
* Annual narrative reports.
* Final report within 90 days.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Ethics Review Process
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Step-by-step guide to the ethics review process.

**Step 1: Pre-submission**
* Check if ethics review is required.
* Complete self-assessment checklist.
* Consult with Ethics Officer.

**Step 2: Application**
* Complete ethics application form.
* Attach all supporting documents.
* Submit via portal.

**Step 3: Review**
* Initial screening (5 days).
* Full committee review (14 days).
* Possible revisions requested.

**Step 4: Decision**
* Approved, approved with conditions, or rejected.
* Appeals process available.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Field Research Safety Protocol
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Safety protocols for all field research expeditions.

**Pre-departure:**
* Medical clearance (all team members).
* Emergency contact registration.
* Insurance verification.
* Safety equipment check.

**During expedition:**
* Daily check-ins with base.
* Weather monitoring.
* Emergency beacon activation procedures.
* First aid kit location.

**Emergency contacts:**
* Base: +44 20 7946 0101
* Insurance: +44 20 7946 0200
* Local embassy: varies by location.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Permit Application Guidelines
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
How to apply for research permits in various jurisdictions.

**General Requirements:**
* Research proposal summary.
* CV of principal investigator.
* Institutional affiliation.
* Insurance certificate.
* Data management plan.

**Timeline:**
* Apply minimum 6 months before fieldwork.
* Some permits require 12 months.
* Expedited processing available for emergencies ($500 fee).

**Common Permits:**
* UNESCO World Heritage sites.
* National parks.
* Marine protected areas.
* Indigenous lands.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Impact Metrics and Reporting
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
How to measure and report research impact.

**Quantitative Metrics:**
* Publications (peer-reviewed, books, chapters).
* Citations (Scopus, Web of Science, Google Scholar).
* H-index.
* Policy citations.
* Media mentions.

**Qualitative Metrics:**
* Policy changes influenced.
* Technology transfer.
* Public engagement.
* Capacity building.
* Scientific breakthroughs.

**Reporting Tools:**
* Altmetric.
* Dimensions.
* Plum Analytics.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Grant Compliance Checklist
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Checklist for maintaining grant compliance.

**Financial:**
* Quarterly reports submitted on time.
* Budget variance < 10%.
* No unallowable expenses.
* Audit trail maintained.

**Scientific:**
* Milestones achieved.
* Publications acknowledged.
* Data sharing compliance.
* IP management.

**Administrative:**
* Institutional approval current.
* Insurance valid.
* Permits current.
* Team members compliant.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Science Communication Best Practices
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Guidelines for communicating research to the public.

**Principles:**
* Accuracy first.
* Avoid jargon.
* Use analogies.
* Show, don't just tell.
* Be honest about limitations.

**Channels:**
* Press releases.
* Social media (Twitter/X, LinkedIn, Instagram).
* Blog posts.
* Podcasts.
* YouTube videos.

**Approval:**
* All press releases must be approved by Communications Officer.
* PI approval required for scientific claims.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Equipment Procurement Process
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Process for procuring research equipment.

**Under $5,000:**
* Direct purchase.
* Three quotes required.
* Purchase order from Finance.

**$5,000 - $50,000:**
* Competitive bidding.
* Minimum 3 vendors.
* Technical evaluation.
* Approval from Financial Compliance.

**Over $50,000:**
* Formal tender process.
* Independent evaluation committee.
* Board approval required.
* 6-month procurement timeline.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: International Research Collaboration
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Guidelines for international research partnerships.

**Agreement Types:**
* MOU (Memorandum of Understanding).
* Collaboration Agreement.
* Data Sharing Agreement.
* IP Joint Ownership Agreement.

**Key Considerations:**
* Export control (dual-use technology).
* Data protection (GDPR, etc.).
* IP ownership.
* Publication rights.
* Conflict of interest.

**Approval:**
* Legal review mandatory.
* Board approval for agreements >$100k.

KnowledgeBaseArticles.Title: Fellowship Application Evaluation Criteria
KnowledgeBaseArticles.Content:
Criteria for evaluating fellowship applications.

**Scientific Merit (40%):**
* Research quality.
* Innovation.
* Feasibility.
* Methodology.

**Candidate Potential (30%):**
* Academic record.
* Research experience.
* Letters of recommendation.
* Career trajectory.

**Impact (20%):**
* Potential contribution to field.
* Broader impacts.
* Capacity building.

**Diversity (10%):**
* Underrepresented groups.
* Geographic diversity.
* Interdisciplinary approach.
